title: Mustardy Crisp Peruvian Chicken Aji-de-gallina
description: Indulgent Peruvian dish featuring crispy pretzel-crusted chicken, creamy mustard sauce, and aji amarillo peppers, all in a rich and creamy sauce made with the classic Peruvian dish, Aji de Gallina. This dish is a Jain-friendly twist on the original recipe, featuring a sous-vide cooking method to ensure food safety and precision cooking.

ingredients.name: pretzels
ingredients.notes: preferably plain, soft pretzels for best results

ingredients.name: mustard seeds
ingredients.notes: brown or black mustard seeds for best flavor

ingredients.name: ají amarillo peppers
ingredients.notes: roasted and pureed aji amarillo peppers for best flavor

instructions: Preheat the sous-vide water bath to 130°F (54°C).
Season the chicken breasts with salt and pepper.
In a shallow dish, mix together the flour, mustard seeds, cumin, garlic powder, and onion powder.
Dip each chicken breast in the flour mixture, coating both sides evenly.
Dip the floured chicken breasts in the beaten eggs and then coat in crushed pretzels, pressing gently to adhere.
Place the coated chicken breasts in a sous-vide bag and seal.
Cook the chicken in the sous-vide water bath for 2 hours.
While the chicken is cooking, roast the aji amarillo peppers in a hot oven until charred and blistered.
Puree the roasted peppers in a blender or food processor until smooth.
In a saucepan, melt the butter over medium heat.
Add the pureed peppers, heavy cream, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Simmer the sauce over medium heat until thickened.
To serve, place a cooked chicken breast on each plate and spoon the creamy pepper sauce over the top.
